topPlaces.name: Tripuranthakam
topPlaces.description: Tripuranthakam is a village in Markapuram district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is the mandal headquarters of Tripuranthakam mandal in Markapur revenue division.

topPlaces.name: Chandavaram Buddhist site
topPlaces.description: Chandavaram Buddhist site is an ancient Indian Buddhist site in Chandavaram village in Prakasam district in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. Situated on the bank of Gundlakamma River, the site is 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) northwest of Donakonda railway station.

items.note: Tripuranthakam is a village in Markapuram district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is the mandal headquarters of Tripuranthakam mandal in Markapur revenue division.

Chandavaram Buddhist site is an ancient Indian Buddhist site in Chandavaram village in Prakasam district in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. Situated on the bank of Gundlakamma River, the site is 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) northwest of Donakonda railway station.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thiripurantakesvararar Temple, Tripuranthakam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thiripurantakesvararar Temple, Tripuranthakam is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thiripurantakesvararar Temple, Tripuranthakam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kurnool Airport (151 km) and the nearest railway station is Guntur Junction (112 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thiripurantakesvararar Temple, Tripuranthakam at a relaxed pace.
